digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design standard and skill integration
propositions: Create a canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals, with the scale-to-the-bug principle explicit.

Ship byte-identical real-file copies of test-design.md in the rebar-debug, rebar-implement, and rebar-brainstorm directories using the same-directory companion-file pattern, with no symlinks or machine-specific paths.

Add a gating unit test test_skill_shared_sync.py that asserts each per-skill copy is byte-identical to the canonical file and self-verifies its detection logic against a perturbed copy under make test.

Integrate the shared file into rebar-debug (Step 4 retitled to RED-oracle design, Step 5 defect-seeded mutation and contract-card handoff), rebar-implement (Phase 4a/4b citations), and rebar-brainstorm (before-recording-tickets read trigger).
Qualify llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md's Phase 2 Repair bullet to cite test-design.md's quantified-eval requirements so the RED-artifact standards cannot disagree.
Document the canonical-plus-copies convention, sync one-liner, and drift gate in the README, and enforce positive-first phrasing with short prohibitions and no negative-example blocks in the prompts.
